prompt: A vintage Hong Kong cinema tribute poster in elegant 1980s–1990s magazine style, featuring a glamorous close-up half-body portrait of Rosamund Kwan seated indoors against a soft neutral beige background. She has voluminous dark brown wavy shoulder-length hair with a side part, luminous skin, a poised gentle expression, one hand raised lightly near her cheek, and wears a creamy white blazer with large round pearl earrings. Use warm diffused studio lighting, soft focus, refined film-like texture, subtle grain, and luxurious nostalgic color grading in beige, ivory, sepia, and deep plum. Design the composition like a memorial or star-profile poster with typography integrated around the portrait: on the far left, very large vertical Chinese calligraphy-style name text in deep plum reading 關之琳; to its right, a slim vertical line of smaller Chinese text reading 絕代佳人・風華永恆. Below the large title on the left, place the English name in elegant italic serif script reading Rosamund Kwan. Under that, add a two-line Chinese quote in smaller serif text. In the lower left corner, include exactly 3 stacked rectangular film still thumbnails with thin borders, each showing the same actress in different roles; each thumbnail has a narrow vertical Chinese caption strip on its left edge. On the lower right beside the portrait, place a text block with the large Chinese surname and given name split across two lines, the English name beneath, then a birth line and 2 more lines of Chinese descriptive text. Add a handwritten autograph-style signature in dark plum near the bottom right. Along the bottom center, add 2 lines of Chinese slogan text in classic serif style, separated from the main layout with ample negative space. Overall look: classy, sentimental, high-end editorial poster, authentic retro Hong Kong actress homage, balanced typography, premium print design, portrait-dominant layout.
